The average fund of everyone, including the 72 lakhs fund paid by the chairman (assuming he's not a member of the club) is P + Q. If P and Q are in integer lakhs, find the possible numerical value of P + Q.

A set of consecutive positive integers beginning with 1 to n is written on the blackboard. A student came along and erased one number. The average of the remaining numbers is 445/17. What was the number erased?

Averages & Alligations.title: AIM 99+ IN CAT 2025 | ACE AVERAGES TEST - 6

questions.stem_text: In a school, the average weights of students in the junior (5th to 7th) grade and senior (8th - 10th) grade divisions are 41 and 57, respectively, and the average weights of all the students in both divisions combined is an integer. If the number of junior students is 8 more than that of the senior students, then the difference between the maximum and minimum possible number of students in the school is.
If ‘x’ is the median of the integers {12, 14, 4, 9, 7, 19, 2, 5, 28, 18, x}, which of the following could possibly be average of all values of the set?

options.text: More than one of the above

questions.stem_text: Hundred students of a class appeared for a test that had 30 questions. Each question carried 1 mark for correct answer and there were no negative markings for wrong answers. The number of students in the class who attempted at least n questions wrong is given by Kn = 60 – 2n (n > 0). Find the average marks scored by the class.

Q 1. Mr Ravi a mathematician told Ujwal, a student, to pick an odd number (say N) less than 8 and write it on the board. Then he asked to perform the following operations:(1) If N is a perfect square, find its positive square root. Write it on the board.(2) If N is not a perfect square:(a)Add N to the number. Write the number on the board.(b) If the new number is a perfect square, find its positive square root. Write it on the board.(c) yIf it is not a perfect square repeat the process from 2(a).At any point if the same set of numbers is repeating, the student can stop the operation.Find the average of all the unique numbers obtained by the children.

There are 6 students in a group. When the heaviest student leaves the group the average weight of the new group reduces by 5 kg. When the lightest student leaves the group the average of the new group increases by 5 kg. If both of them leave the group, then the sum of the weights of the remaining students in the group was 160 kg. What is the weight (in kg) of the heaviest student in the original group?

In a group with a certain number of people, if one new person weighing 75 kg is added, then the average weight of the group increases by 4 kg. If one more person weighing 70 kg is added, then the average weight of the group increases by an additional 3 kg. What is the average weight (in kg) of the original group?

In a class, 40% of the students passed the National Science Olympiad, and the rest failed. Among the students who passed, the highest score achieved was 95, and the lowest score was 42. Among the students who failed, the highest score achieved was 36, and the lowest score was 14. What is the possible range of average score for the entire class (in nearest integers)

Ravi finds the average of five two-digit numbers. If one number is reversed and the average is taken again, then the average increase by 3.6. If all five numbers are consecutive multiples of five, then find the number which is reversed.

A dishonest shopkeeper has two varieties of Rice. One variety costs Rs. 12 per kilogram and the other variety costs Rs. 15 per kilogram. He mixes the two varieties in the ratio 6 : 11. Further, he adds 24 kg of adulterant to the mixture to gain a higher profit percentage. If the shopkeeper makes a profit of 40% by selling the adulterated mixture at the average cost of the Rice mixture, what is the nearest integer total amount (in Rs.) realised by selling the adulterated mixture?
The average of 30 whole numbers is 6.4. The maximum number of distinct numbers among these 30 whole numbers can be:

Averages & Alligations.title: AIM 99+ IN CAT 2025 | ACE AVERAGES TEST - 7

questions.stem_text: The average of 4 distinct prime numbers a, b, c, d is 35, where a < b < c < d. a and d are equidistant from 36 and b and c are equidistant from 34 and a, b are equidistant from 30 and c and d are equidistant from 40. The Sum of a and d is:

Eleven years earlier the average age of a family of 4 members was 29 years. Now the age of the same family with six members is yet the same, even when 2 children were born in this period. If they belong to the same parents and the age of the first child at the time of the birth of the younger child was same as there were total family members just after the birth of the youngest members of this family, then the present age of the youngest member of the family is:

In a Mock CFA exam, 125 students appeared and the average score obtained was 122. But later it was found that the top three students were repeaters, so their score was eliminated and then the new average score was found to be decreased by 4.5. Also, it is known that all the students obtained the marks in integers and the scores of the toppers were distinct. If the second-highest topper has scored more than 300 marks, then the highest possible score of the third-highest topper is:

10 years ago the average age of all the 25 teachers of the Girl’s college was 46, 5 years ago the principal retired from her post at the age of 62. So after one year a new principal whose age was 56 years was recruited The present average age of all the teachers is, if principal also considered as a teacher:

If p, q, r be three positive numbers such that p > q > r when the smallest number is added to the difference of the rest two numbers, then the average of the resultant number and the original numbers except to the smallest number is 24 more than the average of all the three original numbers. The value of (p – q) is:

The average weight of all the 11 players of the Indian Hockey team is 54 kg. If the average of first six lightest weight players is 48 kg and that of the six heaviest players is 60 kg. The weight of the player which lies in the sixth position in the list of players when all the 11 players are arranged in the order of increasing or decreasing weights is:

Arvind and Chintan are among 10 students who wrote the FMS exam. Arvind scores 10. The average score of the 9 students other than Chintan is 6. The average score of all the 10 students is 0.5 more than the average score of the 9 students other than Arvind. The score of Chintan is:

In a planet of Dwarfs, The average height of 32 dwarves increases by 4 inches when two of them leave this group. If the average height of these two dwarves is one-third the average height of the original 32, then the average height, in inches, of the remaining 30 dwarves is:

Dimony appears for a certain number of tests. His average score increases by 1 if the first 10 tests are not considered, and decreases by 1 if the last 10 tests are not considered. If his average scores for the first 10 and the last 10 tests are 20 and 30, respectively, then the Sum of the total number of tests taken by him and the average of those tests is:
The mean of six positive integers is 15. The median is 18, and the only mode of the integers is less than 18. The maximum possible value of the largest of the six integers is:

Averages & Alligations.title: AIM 99+ IN CAT 2025 | ACE MIXTURES AND ALLIGATION TEST - 1

questions.stem_text: In what proportion can three varieties of sugar priced at Rs. 36 per kg, Rs. 52 per kg and Rs. 60 per kg respectively be mixed, so that the price of the mixture is Rs. 48 per kg?

Two varieties of tea costing Rs. 420 per kg and Rs. 540 per kg are mixed in a certain ratio to from varieties P and Q. P and Q are mixed in the ratio 4 : 3 to from a variety R which is sold for Rs. 690 per kg at 43.75% profit. If P cost price is Rs. 450 per kg, then is what ratio were the two types of teas mixed to form the varieties Q?

There are three vessels P, Q and R, each having a capacity of 1000 ml. All the vessels are fully filled. Each vessel contains a mixture of salt and water. Salt concentration of P, Q and R is 20%, 44% and 64% respectively. Now 200 ml of solution in vessel P is transferred to Q, then 200 ml of solution in vessel Q is transferred to vessel R and finally then 200 ml of solution in vessel R is transferred to P. Find the salt concentration in vessel P at the end of these three operations.

A can contains 72 liters of pure milk. At first, 9 liters of milk is withdrawn and replaced with 9 liters of water. At second, 8 liters of mixture is withdrawn and replaced with 8 liters of water. At third, 18 liters of mixture is withdrawn and replaced with 18 liters of milk. Find the quantity of milk at the end of these three operations.
Fresh dates contains 84% water, while dry dates contains 36% water. How many kg of dry dates can be obtained from 48 kg of fresh dates?

A business man lends Rs. 36000 in two parts, one at 18.75% of simple interest for 1 year and other at 31.25% of compound interest with interest being compound annually for 1 year. If the average interest earned in 1 year is 20%, then find the value (in Rs.) of the part which is lend at 18.75% of simple interest.

Rashi, a rice trader mixes two varieties of rice such that 5 times the cost price of first variety is equal to 8 times the cost price of second variety. If Rashi sells the mixture at the cost price of costlier variety of rice and thus makes a profit of 25%, then find the ratio in which first variety and second variety of rice are mixed?

A local merchant mixed three varieties of tea T1, T2 and T3 priced at Rs. 632 per kg, Rs. 512 per kg and Rs. 496 per kg respectively in the ratio 1 : 2 : 4. To 5 kg of this mixture he adds T1 and T3 in the ratio 2 : 3 and now sells this entire mixture for Rs. 680.40 per kg, thereby making a profit of 25%. How much of T1 (in kg) did he mix with the mixture?

Rohan and Sohan both buys milk at Rs. 60 per liter. Both mixes the milk with water, which is free of cost. Rohan and Sohan sells the mixture at Rs. 50 per liter and Rs. 72 per liter respectively. If the profit percent of Rohan and Sohan are 20% and 25% respectively, then find the ratio of water added per liter of pure milk by Rohan and Sohan respectively. Assume that water is free of cost.

There are 5 fully filled cans of equal volumes. Each can contains a solution of milk and water. The ratio of milk to water in these cans are 3 : 2, 5 : 3, 2 : 7, 7 : 5 and 8 : 7. If all the solutions in these 5 cans are mixed, then find the ratio of water to milk is the final mixture.

Averages & Alligations.title: AIM 99+ IN CAT 2025 | ACE MIXTURES AND ALLIGATION TEST - 2

questions.stem_text: A fully filled beaker contains milk and water in the ratio 7 : 5. If a person removes 10 liters of this mixture and replaces it with water, the ratio of milk to water in the beaker now becomes 4 : 3. Find the capacity of the beaker (in liters).

Three milkmen Miku, Piiku and Riku had 8 liters, 11 liters and 14 liters of milk-water solutions respectively. In the solutions Piku and Riku had, the ratio of milk and water were 6 : 5 and 4 : 3 respectively. Now they sat together and mixed all of their respective solutions. They sold the mixture at Rs. 64 per liter and Miku got Rs. 8. for every liter of mixture sold. What was the ratio of milk and water in Miku solution, if the revenue earned is shared in the ratio of milk content in respective solution and water is free of cost?

A vessel of capacity K liters is fully filled with solution of milk and water. Initially the milk concentration is 40%. Now, 45 liters of mixture is withdrawn and replaced with 45 liters of water. Then, 40 liters of mixture is withdrawn and replaced with 40 liters of water. If the quantity of water in the mixture after these two operations is 248 liters, then find the value of K.

Container A and Container B contains Sugar, milk power and water in the ratio of 2 : 3 : 5 and 3 : 5 : 8 respectively. Some parts from container A and container B are thoroughly mixed and put into another container C. Which of the following cannot be the ratio of sugar, milk and water in the container C?

2K liters of solutions having x% alcohol is mixed with 3K liters of solution having y% alcohol to give 72% alcohol solution. If both x and y are positive integers and x > y, then how many values of x are possible?

There is an alloy (P) of silver and zinc. A certain weight of this alloy is mixed with 24 kg of pure silver and melted to form the new alloy (Q), which contains 84% of silver. Instead if the alloy (P) is mixed with 18 kg of silver and zinc alloy having 90% silver, then the new alloy(R) is formed, which contains 80% silver. Find the quantity of silver (in kg) in alloy P.

There are two steel alloys each having different percentage of steel. First one of them weighs 18 kg and second one weighs 24 kg. One piece each of equal weight was cut off from both the alloys. The piece of first alloy was mixed with the leftover part of second alloy and the piece of second alloy was mixed with the left over part of first alloy. As a result, the percentage of steel became same in both the alloys. What was the weight of each cut out piece?

A container has 64 liters of pure milk. 8 liters are removed from the container and replaced with 8 liters of water. This process of replacing 8 liters of liquid in the container with equal volume of water is continued repeatedly. The smallest number of times of doing this process, after which the volume of water in the container becomes more than 50% is?

100 liters of water is added to milk and water solution such that after this addition, milk concentration in the mixture becomes 30%. Further 120 liters of water is added to this mixture and the concentration of milk in the mixture now reduced by 5% points. How many more liters of water must be this mixture such that concentration of milk become 18%?

Rohan mixes coca with jaggery in the ratio 5 : 3 to prepare mixture P, and coffee with jaggery in the ratio 9 : 7 to prepare the mixture Q. He combines mixture P and Q in the ratio 3 : 2 to make a new mixture R. If he mixes R with an equal amount of milk to create a drink, the percentage of jiggery in this final mixture is?

Averages & Alligations.title: AIM 99+ IN CAT 2025 | ACE MIXTURES AND ALLIGATION TEST - 3

questions.stem_text: If 6k liters of solution A having a% spirit, 9k liters of solution B having b% spirit, and 12k liters of solution C having (a + b)% spirit are all mixed in a container, the resultant solution has 48% spirit. If 100 liters of A and 200 liters of B are mixed, then the maximum possible spirit (in liters) in this mixture is how many liters more than the minimum possible spirit (in liters) in the mixture? Given that a and b are positive integers.

Two varieties of rice, polished rice and brown rice are mixed in the ratio 5 : 7 respectively and the mixture is sold at ₹ 69 per kg at a profit of 43.75%. If the cost of brown rice per kg is ₹ 12 more than the cost of polished rice per kg, then the cost price of polished rice is approximately how much percent less than the cost price of brown rice?

Bottle 1 and bottle 2 both contains the solutions of milk and water. The ratio of milk and water in bottle 1 and bottle 2 are in the ratio 79 : 71 and 91 : 59 respectively. The contents of bottle 1 and bottle 2 are mixed in the ratio P : Q such that the mixture contains 58% milk. If the contents of bottle 1 and bottle 2 are mixed in the ratio 3P : 2Q and the ratio of milk and water in this final mixture is A : B (where A and B are co-prime), find the value of (A + B).

Rohan wanted to give up drinking in the next 25 days. So, he purchased a 900 ml bottle of Cobra whisky. On day 1, he drank 4% of the whisky and replaced it with water. Next day, he drank 8% of the contents of the bottle and replaced it with water. On the third day he drank 12% of the contents of the bottle and replaced it with water. Like this on the 25th day he drank 100% of the contents, threw away the bottle, and stopped drinking. Find out how much volume of water (in ml) Rohan had to drink with the whisky to give up drinking?

A mixture comprises water and liquids P and Q. The volume of water is 40% of the total volume of mixture and the volume of liquids P and Q are in the ratio 7 : 5. To remove the water, the mixture is passed through a porous medium which completely absorbs the water and partially absorbs liquid P. Altogether this porous medium absorbs 250 ml of initial mixture. If the ratio of volume of liquids P and Q in the residual concentrated mixture becomes 1 : 2, then find the volume of water (in ml) absorbed by the porous medium.

A container contains P liters of milk. A thief stole 75 litres of milk and replaced it with same quantity of water. He repeated the same process further two times. After the three operations, the quantity of milk left in the container is only (P – 183) liters. Find the value of P, given that P is a positive integer. Also, given that P is a multiple of 25.

A mixture of water and milk has 36% milk in it. It is known that, if 18 liters of the mixture is replaced with 18 liters of water, the milk percentage would decrease to 27%. Instead, how many liters of the mixture should be replaced with water, for the milk percentage to decrease to 18%?

Sixty liters of a mixture of milk and water contains 40% water. This mixture is added to Ninety litres of another mixture that contains 60% of water. Then, how many liters of water should be added to the resulting mixture to obtain a final mixture that contains 76% water?

There are two containers of the same volume, which is 2 times the filled volume. First container is half filled with pure milk and the second container is half filled with water. Two-third of the contents of first container is transferred to the second container, and then two-third of conduct of this mixture is transferred back to the first container. Next, two-third of the first container is transferred back to the second container. Then, the ratio of water to milk in the second container is?

An alloy A has copper and zinc in the ratio 5 : 3 (by weight), while another alloy B has the same metals in the ratio of 2 : 5 (by weight). If these two alloys are mixed in the ratio P : Q (by weight), a new alloy C is formed, which has equal contents of copper and zinc. Then the ratio of copper and zinc in the alloy D, formed by mixing the two alloys A and B in the ratio Q : P (by weight) is?

Time, Speed & Distance.title: AIM 99+ IN CAT 2025 | ACE Boats and Streams - 1

questions.stem_text: Ram walked down a descending escalator and took 60 steps to reach the bottom. Shyam starts simultaneously from the bottom, taking 5 steps to every 2 steps taken by Ram. Time taken by Ram to reach the bottom from the top is the same as the time taken by Shyam to reach the top from bottom. When they crossed each other, Shyam took how many steps more than Ram?

In a descending escalator having 360 steps, Ram is running upwards and Shyam is running downwards at different speeds. Ratio of speeds of Ram and Shyam is 7 : 2. The speed of the escalator is the difference of twice the speed of Ram and six times the speed of Shyam. Find the number of steps from bottom, where both Ram and Shyam met on the escalator.

A man walks up an ascending escalator at a speed of 5 steps per second and reaches the top in 40 steps. If he walks up the ascending escalator at 8 steps per second, he reaches the top in 56 steps. Find the number of steps on the stationary escalator.

Time taken by a person to reach the first floor from the ground floor on a moving up escalator is 18 sec less than that time taken when the escalator is turned off. Again, the time taken by the same person to reach ground floor from the first floor on the same moving up escalator is 45 sec more composed to when the escalator is turned off. If the speed of the person and escalator are in the ratio 7 : 3, find the time (in sec) taken by person to reach first floor from ground floor, when he stand still on moving up escalator.

A man can row a certain distance upstream in 24 min more than he could row in still water. Also, he can row the same distance downstream in 18 min less time than he could row in still water. Find the time taken(min) by the man to row the same distance downstream if his rowing speed becomes twice and speed of stream becomes 4 times the usual stream speed.

Rohan takes 17 seconds to finish riding an ascending escalator if he walks at twice of his normal speed in the same direction as escalator. He takes 12 seconds to finish riding the same escalator if he walks at thrice of his normal speed in the same direction as escalator. If Rohan decides to stand still on the ascending escalator, the time (in sec) needed to finish riding the escalator is?.

Boat A takes 5 hours to travel downstream in a river from point P to point Q and takes 9 hours to return back to point P from Q. Another boat B takes a total of 54 hours to travel from point P to Q and then return back to point P. Find the time taken, (in hours) by boat B to travel from Q to P.

Mohan and Sohan starts simultaneously from Haridwar and Kanpur respectively on their motorboats and they are going towards Kanpur and Haridwar respectively. They both reached their destinations and they came back towards their starting point. Speed of the Mohan’s and Sohan’s motorboat in still water is 48 km/h and 60 km/h respectively. Speed of the stream is 12 km/h and direction of flow of stream is from Haridwar to Kanpur. If the distance between Kanpur and Haridwar is 600 km. At what distance (in km) from Kanpur they will meet for the second time?

A wooden block floating downstream along the river can cover a distance of D km in 24 hours. A boat rider with its usual speed can cover D km upstream and then D km downstream in a total of 10 hours. However, if the speed of the boat is increased by 40%, it takes boat rider a total of 7 hours to travel D km downstream and the D km upstream. Find the time taken (in hours) by boat rider to travel D km upstream with usual speed of the boat.

Rohan has a motorboat, whose speed is 39 km/h in still water. One day Rohan first rode the boat 240 km upstream and then he came back 240 km downstream. Upstream time was 3 hours more than the downstream time. On another day, Rohan went N km upstream and then came back N km downstream, spending a total time of at least 6.5 hours. Find the minimum integral value of N.

Time, Speed & Distance.title: AIM 99+ IN CAT 2025 | ACE Time AND Work - 1

questions.stem_text: The refined Ten men and six women can finish a project in 18 days. Men work 9 hours a day, whereas women only work 7.5 hours. The effectiveness of a woman per hour is only 2/3 that of a male. How many days does it take 10 men and 9 women to finish the job?
Men resolved to complete a task in 4 days. However, because 20 workers quit every day, the job was finished in 7 days. How many men were present at first?

Ajith, Chinmay, and Niraj together can do a piece of work in 36 days. Ajith and Chinmay together can do thrice the work as Niraj, whereas Ajith and Niraj together can do four times the work as Chinmay in the same time. Find the time taken(in days) by Niraj alone to do the same work.

A cistern can be filled by two pipelines A and B independently in 15 and 10 minutes, respectively, while a waste pipe C can remove 7 L per minute. When the cistern is full and all the pipes are opened, it empties in 2 hours. What volume of water can the cistern hold?

364 taps needed repair by four plumbers. They had repaired 168 taps after three days of working seven hours per day. Three more plumbers were enlisted who also worked seven hours each day to speed up the work. How many additional days would it take to finish the assignment if the efficiency of each plumber is the same?

A tank can be filled by pipe A in 8 minutes and by pipe B in 10 minutes. The tank may be drained in 12 minutes using outlet pipe C. Pipes A and C were opened for one-half of the time while pipes B and C were opened for the other half of the time. This process was repeated to fill an empty tank. The time it takes long tank to be fully filled is a/b what is a+b?
X, Y, and Z can complete a task in 20, 30, and 60 days, respectively. If Y and Z assist X every third day. How much time(in days) will it take them to finish the work?

Two pipes A and B can independently fill a cistern in 45 minutes and 35 minutes, respectively. The full cistern can be emptied in 30 minutes with a tap C at the bottom. At approximately what time(in min) from the opening of pipelines A and B does the cistern fill up if tap C is opened 7 minutes after the two pipes A and B are opened?
500 units can be produced in 12 hours using 14 machines. A further two machines were started after four hours of production. How many hours will it take to produce 500 units in total?
A water tank ordinarily takes 7 hours to fill from a faucet, but the leak adds an additional 2 hours to the process. How long would it take a leak to completely drain a full water tank?

Time, Speed & Distance.title: AIM 99+ IN CAT 2025 | ACE Time AND Work - 2

questions.stem_text: Consider three friends A, B and C who work at differing speeds. When the slowest two work together they take n days to finish a task. When the quickest two work together they take m days to finish a task. One of them, if he worked alone would take thrice as much time as it would take when all three work together. How much time would it take if all three worked together?

A can build a wall in 5 days B can build a wall in 15 days and C can destroy a wall in 10 days. They work in the sequence of B C A that is on first day b worked then on second day c then 3rd day a. In how much days will the work be completed

Prithvi takes half as much time as Ashok to build a wall. Ashok takes half as much time as Jayant to build the wall. Prashant takes 3/4th as much time as Jayant takes to build the wall. The ratio of time required for Prithvi and Jayant (when they work together) to build the wall to the time required for Ashok and Prashant (when they work together) to build the wall is a:b find a+b.

If the efficiency of 2 women and 3 men is less than that of 4 children and 1 man and if the efficiency of 3 women is less than that of 2 children, then how many of the given statements are correct?(I) The efficiency of man is more than that of a child.(II) The combined efficiency of a man and woman is less than that of two children.(III)The combined efficiency of a man and a woman is more than that of two children.

A drain pipe can drain a tank in 12 hours, and a fill pipe can fill the same tank in 6 hours. A total of n pipes – which include a few fill pipes and the remaining drain pipes – can fill the entire tank in 2 hours. How many of the following values could ‘n’ take among the following( 24 16 33 13 9 8)

A large tank of height 10 m is fitted with an inlet pipe which can fill the tank in 60 minutes. The tank has three emptying outlets pipes fitted at the heights of 5 m, 8 m and 9.5 m respectively from the bottom. All the pipes are opened simultaneously with the tank being empty initially. In how much time (in minutes) will 95% of the tank be filled, if the lowest outlet pipe alone can empty half the tank in one and a half hour; the outlet pipe in the middle alone can empty 20% of the tank in 1 hour 12 minutes and the outlet pipe at the top alone can empty 5% of the tank in 18 minutes (round off to the nearest integer)

Work involving shifting of heavy machine pieces had to be completed in 50 days. Only one male worker worked on the first day. A woman joined him on the second day. One more male worker joined them on the third day. One more woman joined them on the fourth day. This process continued till the 40th day and the job was completed within scheduled time. Three women together perform as much work as being done by two men. How many days(to nearest integer) will 30 women take to complete the job?

While working alone, Raja takes 5 more days to complete a job than the number of days taken by Kumar alone. Initially, both Raja and Kumar started working together, but Kumar left work after some days. After that, Raja took 5 days to complete the remaining part of the job. If Raja completed 60% of the job, how many days will Kumar take to complete the same job, if he works alone?

An outlet pipe can empty a completely filled tank in 16 hours and an inlet pipe can completely fill an empty tank in 10 hours. A total of n number of inlet and p number of outlet pipes are connected with the tank. The empty tank becomes full in 4 hours when all the inlet and outlet pipes work together. Which of the following cannot be the value of (n + p)?

Two workers are filling an empty vessel. The capacity of the vessel is 50 cups. Each worker pours a cup of liquid to it in one minute. One worker adds milk while the other naughty worker adds a cup of water after every two cups of milk that he adds. If two workers add a cup of milk/water to the vessels one cup at a time in turns, how many cups of milk will be in the vessel when the vessel is half filled?

Time, Speed & Distance.title: AIM 99+ IN CAT 2025 | ACE Time AND Work - 3

questions.stem_text: A tank is connected by 12 pipelines. While some are drain pipes, while others are fill pipes. Each fill pipe can fill the tank in 8 hours, and each drain pipe can entirely empty the tank in 6 hours. A tank that is empty will be filled in 3 hours if all the lines are kept open. Of the 12 pipes, how many are fill pipes?

A task can be completed in 30 days by 33 men. Suppose a job was started by 44 men, and one man left for the day after each day’s work. How many days at the very least must be spent on the entire project?

Pipes A and B together can fill a water tank in 36 hours, pipes B and C together can fill the same tank in 24 hours, and pipes A and C together can fill the same tank in 30 hours. If all the three pipes are opened together, then they fill 74 litres of water per minute. Find the capacity of the tank (in litres).
A can do 30% of a job in 36 hours. B is 25% more efficient than A. C is 20% less efficient than B. How long(in hours to nearest integer) would the three of them take to do the job together?

Three factories are capable of manufacturing a particular part of the car. Each of the two factories can produce 200, 000 parts in 30 days. The third factory can produce two times the number of parts manufactured by each of these two factories. How many days will the three factories take to produce 1.6 million parts together?

While doing some work, the efficiency of A is equal to the average of one third of the efficiency of C and thrice the efficiency of B. The efficiency of B is equal to the average of the one fifth efficiency of C and half the efficiency of A. The ratio of the efficiencies of A and B is k:p find absolute difference between k and p,also both k and p are co primes?

Alok can complete a task in 45 days, however, Brijesh can complete the same task alone in 5 days fewer time than Alok. Alok and Brijesh both started the job together, but after a few days, Brijesh left. Alok completed the remaining work in 56 days with just half of his usual efficiency, whereas he worked with Brijesh at full capacity. How long did they collaborate for?

Time, Speed & Distance.title: AIM 99+ IN CAT 2025 | ACE Time AND Work - 4

questions.stem_text: A tank can be topped off and drained using a booster pump. The tank has an 8000 m3 capacity. The pump’s capacity for emptying is 110 m3 per minute greater than its capacity for loading, and it takes 110 minutes less for the pump to empty the tank than it does to fill it. What is the pump’s capacity for filling?

A swimming pool can be filled by two taps P and Q in 8t and 6t hours, respectively. In the same amount of time that P and Q need to fill the pool together, another tap, R, can empty it. The swimming pool will be filled in 40 hours if taps P and Q are always open and tap R is opened for one hour after every four hours. How many hours does it take tap R to empty a full swimming pool?

Two pipes A and B together can fill a tank in 16 minutes. On a particular day, pipe A is opened in an empty tank and as soon as the tank is half full, this pipe is closed and pipe B is opened and thus, the tank is filled in a total of 32 minutes. Now, if there is a leak in the tank because of which pipe A takes 40 minutes to fill the tank, then how much time will pipe B alone take to fill the tank

Naren alone can code the identical program in six days shorter than Gani, while Gani alone can code it in B days. In 45 days, Pawan can finish the same task by himself. They all got to work together, and after 12 days, the program was finished. How many days will Naren need to finish the program’s coding?

While working independently, A and B can finish a task in x days and (x – y) days where y is an odd composite number that is more than 16 but less than 25, and that is the sum of two prime numbers. The total number of days required to accomplish the task will be an odd number and will be the same regardless of who starts the work if A and B work on alternating days with A working with 100% higher efficiency and B working with 16.66% less efficiency. Discover x’s potential value.

The efficiency gap between A and B while performing any task is 300% of A’s efficiency. B needs 15 extra days to complete a task than C. B and C started the work, and after certain days, A joined the work and B left. The task would have been finished in just 1 more day if this had occurred five days earlier. How long will it take A to do the task alone.

The rate of flow of water (in liter per min) of three pipes are 2, N and 3, where 2 < N < 3. The lowest and the highest flow rates are both decreased by a certain quantity x, while the intermediate rate is left. unchanged. If the reciprocals of the three flow rates, in the order given above, are in arithmetic progression. both before and after the change, then what is the quantity x (in liter per min)? (Negative flow rates indicate that the pipes act as emptying pipes instead of filing pipes.)

Time, Speed & Distance.title: AIM 99+ IN CAT 2025 | ACE Time AND Work - 5

questions.stem_text: A can do 37.5% of the work in 15 days and B can do 48% of the work in 24 days. A and B along with C started working on the work and they worked together for 10 days. After 10 days, A left and then B and C together completed the remaining work in 15 days. In how many days, C alone will complete the entire work?

144 men working 12 hours a day plans to complete a piece of work in 24 days. 144 men worked for 9 days, per day for k hours and completed only 25% of total work. Now y more men joined and all these men (144 and y extra men) completed the remaining work in 16 more days, working k hours per day. Find the value of y.

Amal and Bimal alone can finish a piece of work in 18 and x days respectively. If they work on alternate days, they can finish the work exactly by the end of the same number of days irrespective of who starts the work. How many values can ‘x’ take if ‘x’ is a positive integer?

X and Y can alone can finish a piece of work in 18 and 24 days respectively. X and Y together worked for 4 days, but thereafter they both fell ill and their efficiency becomes one-fifth of their actual efficiency. To finish the remaining work, Z joined them and all three finished the remaining work in 22 days. Find in how many days Z alone will take to complete the same work.

A can do a piece of work in 3 days. B takes thrice as much time as A to complete the same piece of work. C takes thrice as much time as B to complete the same piece of work. D takes thrice as much time as C to complete the same piece of work and E takes thrice as much time as D to complete the same piece of work. If A’s 1-day work exceed the total 1-day work of B, C, D and E together by 287 units, then C’s 1-day work is how much units more than E’s 1-day work?

At their usual efficiency levels, P and Q together finish a task in 73 days. If P had worked 31.25 % less efficiently as he usually does, and Q had worked 41.66 % more efficiently as he usually does, then the task would have been completed in 68 days. If P works at 175% of his usual efficiency and Q works at 250% of his usual efficiency, then together they will finish the same task in how many days?
5 men and 7 women can complete a piece of work in 40 days. 4 men and 3 women can complete the same work in 60 days. In how many days will 6 men and 11 women can complete thrice the original work?

A person can complete a piece of work in 5461 days. He works alone on Day 1. On every successive Day after Day 1, the number of workers as well as the efficiency of each worker gets doubled as compared to previous day. In how many days will they able to complete the original work?

Amar takes thrice as much time as taken by Akbar and Anthony together to complete a piece of work. Akbar takes twice as much time as taken by Amar and Anthony together to complete the same piece of work. If Anthony alone can complete the work in 21 days, in how many days Amar and Akbar together can complete twice the original work?

Time, Speed & Distance.title: AIM 99+ IN CAT 2025 | ACE Time AND Work - 6

questions.stem_text: A buffalo can graze as much in 8 days as a goat can in 18 days. A cow can graze as much in 15 days as a buffalo can in 12 days. To graze a certain field a goat and a cow together take 101 days. How much time does it take for all the three together to graze the same field?

There are five pipes A, B, C, D, and E which alone can fill an empty tank in 12 min, 18 min, 20 min, 24 min, and 40 min respectively. Exactly two of these pipes are now converted into emptying pipes, while other three remains the filling pipes. Now all these pipes working together can fill an empty tank in 3 hours. Which of the following is not a filling pipe?

A pipe P alone can fill an empty tank in 24 min, and another pipe Q alone can empty the same fully filled tank in 36 min. Both the pipes are attached to a tank and the capacity of the tank is 360 liters. Initially the tank is empty and at any particular time only one of the pipe is open, while the other is closed. Pipe P is closed automatically, when water level in the tank reaches exactly 87.5% of the height of the tank and simultaneously the pipe Q opens and operates until the water level in the tank falls to exactly 12.5% of the height of the tank, after which Q closes automatically and simultaneously the pipe P opens. If the pipe P is opened first and this process is continued indefinitely, how much water (in liters) will be there in the tank at the end of exactly 3 hours?

2 men, 3 women, and 5 children can finish a piece of work in 50 days. 3 men, 5 women, and 7 children can finish the same work in 33 days. 5 men, 5 women, and 8 children can finish the same work in 25 days. Find the number of days in which 8 men, 7 women, and 11 children can finish thrice the original work with their regular efficiency.

A group of men, all having same efficiency, working together can do a job in 18 days. Since the beginning of work, 10 men dropped out at the end of each day, and the job got completed at the end of the 24th day. How many men were there at the beginning?

A tank of capacity 2024 liters, has N taps numbered from 1 to N fitted into it. At the start of the nth minute, for 1 ≤ n ≤ N, the tap number n, which fills the tank at the rate of n liters per min is opened. If it takes exactly N min to fill the tank in this manner, find N.

P takes twice as much time as taken by Q and R together to complete a piece of work. Q takes thrice as much time as taken by P and R together to complete the same piece of work. P, Q, and R together take 20 days to finish the same work. In how many days R alone will finish the same work?

Tap A, tap B, and tap C, when opened simultaneously can fill an empty tank in 30 minutes. If tap A alone can fill 37.5% of the tank in 36 minutes and tap B alone can fill 43.75% of the tank in 42 minutes, how long (in minutes) will it tank for tap C alone to fill 62.5% of the empty tank?

The women are at least 66.66% as efficient as the men, but not more efficient than men. Children are at least half as efficient as the minimum efficiency of women, but not more efficient than the minimum efficiency of women. If the minimum possible number of days taken by 9 men, 6 women and 9 children working together to a certain task are 144, find the maximum possible number of days for 8 men, 9 women and 12 children to do the same task.

A filling pipe can fill an empty tank in 9 hours and a drain pipe can drain the same fully filled tank in 18 hours. A total of k pipes (some filling and other drain) can fill the empty tank in 3 hours. How many values of k >= 2 are possible if k is not more than 50? Note: Number of drain pipes are not zero.

Time, Speed & Distance.title: AIM 99+ IN CAT 2025 | ACE Time AND Work - 7

questions.stem_text: P can complete a task in 8 hours less time than Q takes to complete the same task. If P and Q together can complete the same task in 700 minutes, how long (in hours) does Q alone will take to complete the task?

A tank is connected with both inlet pipes and outlet pipes. Individually, an inlet pipe can fill this empty tank in 18 hours and an outlet pipe can empty this fully filled tank it in 16 hours. If all the pipes are opened simultaneously, then the empty tank gets fully filled in exactly 6 hours. If the total number of pipes (inlet + outlet) are 20, then number of inlet pipes are how much percent more than the number of outlet pipes?

Two pipes alone can fill an empty tank in 24 hours and 36 hours respectively. The pipes are opened together but due to a pipe leakage, it takes 96 minutes extra to fill the tank. If the tank is fully filled, what time (in hours) will it take to completely empty only due to leakage?

A large tank of height 40 m is fitted with an inlet pipe which alone can fill the tank in 80 min. The tank has three emptying outlet pipes fitted at the heights of 16 m, 24 m and 32 m respectively from bottom. All the pipes are opened simultaneously with the tank being empty initially. In how much time (in min) will 87.5% of the tank gets filled, if the outlet pipes at 16 m alone can empty half the tank in 120 minutes, the outlet pipe at 24 m can empty 12.5% of the tank in 45 min and the outlet pipe at 32 m alone can empty 5% of the tank in 12 min?

A task is assigned to a group of 73 men, each having distinct efficiency. Every day exactly two men out of the group work on the task, with no same pair of men working together twice. Even after all the possible pairs have worked once, all the men together had to work for exactly four days more to finish the task. Find the number of days that will be required for all the men working together to finish the same task.

A group of men of same efficiency was employed to shift 1245 crates. Every day after the first, 5 more men than the previous day were put on the job. Also, every day after the first day, each man on job shifts 4 fever crates than the number of crates moved by each man on the previous day. In this way, work was finished in 5 days. If the total number of crates shifted on third day was square of a prime number, then find the number on crated shifted on day 5.

A certain work was completed by A, B and C working together in 20 days. Instead, if the three of them complete the work in such a way that all starts working together and A works for 4 days and then takes rest for next two days, B works for 5 days and then takes rest for next 3 days, whereas C work for 7 days and then takes rest for the next 5 days and they keep working on the job in the same manner until the work gets over. Also, the efficiency of A, B, and C is same. In how many days will the work be completed?

A contractor undertakes to build a wall in 80 days. He hires 108 people for the same. However, after 50 days, he finds that work is only 45% complete. How many more men (of same efficiency as previously hired men) the contractor must hire to complete the work 8 days before the scheduled time of 80 days?

A group of 12 persons containing men and women can do a piece of work in 26 days, other group of 10 persons containing men and women can do the same piece of work in 32 days. Number of men in first group is 1 more than the number of men in second group. If there is another group of 2 men and 6 women which can do the same piece of work in 52 days. Efficiency of 1 woman is how much percent less than the efficiency of 1 man?

3 men, 5 women and 9 children working together can complete a piece of work in 45 days. 5 men, 3 women and 13 children can also complete the same work in 36 days. 15 men, 21 children and 9 women can complete the same work in 15 days. 36 women will complete the same work in how many days?

Time, Speed & Distance.title: AIM 99+ IN CAT 2025 | ACE Time AND Work - 8

questions.stem_text: Three workers T, P and J are appointed to do a job. They started the job together but J left after 12 days when 60% of the job was done. The remaining job was completed by T and P in 10 days. The ratio of efficiency of T and P is 1 : 3. Find the number of days required by the fastest worker to complete 60% of the job alone.

The total number of men, women and children working in a factory is 53.They earn ₹54000 a day in total. The wages of an individual man, woman and child is in the ratio 8 : 5 : 3. If the sum of the wages of all men, all women and all children are in the ratio 4 : 3 : 2, then how much does a woman earn (in ₹) in a day?

A contractor employed a certain number of workers to finish a task in a certain scheduled time. Sometime later, when a part of task had been completed, he realized that work would get delayed by three-fifth of the scheduled time, so he at once increased the number of workers by 80% and thus managed to finish the task on the scheduled time. How much work had been completed before increasing the number of workers?

Mr. Rohan employed N workers for a project. He wanted to complete the project in D days. Initially all N workers worked for 12 days and then 25% of the workers left the project. It was found that it took as much time to complete the work from then (after 12 days) as the entire work needed with all the workers. Find the value of D.

Working together A and B takes 40% more number of days than A, B, and C take together to complete a piece of work. B and C working together takes 10 more days than A, B, and C take together to complete the same piece of work. All three started working together and remained together till the completion of work. If B received ₹ 360 out of total earning of ₹ 945, then in how many days A, B, and C working together will complete the total work?

Arun and Varun alone can do a piece of work in 18 days and 24 days respectively with 100% of their efficiency. They work in such a way that Arun works alone with 100%, 50% and 25% efficiency on Monday, Wednesday, and Friday respectively and Varun works alone with 25%, 50%, and 100% on Tuesday, Thursday, and Saturday respectively. Sunday is off for both. Arun started on the work on 1st March, which was Monday. On which day the work will be completed?

Rohan and Sohan working together can finish a work in 24 days. If Rohan does only 62.5% of his usual work on a day, Sohan must to 143.75% of his usual work on that day to exactly make up for it. Number of days required by the slower worker alone to complete twice the given work is?

There friends P, Q, and R work at different speeds. When the slowest two work together, they take 18 days to finish a task. When the quickest two work together, they take 14 days to finish the task. One of them, if he worked alone take thrice as much time as it would take when all thee work together. How much time (in days) would it take if all three worked together?

Time, Speed & Distance.title: AIM 99+ IN CAT 2025 | ACE Time Speed And Distance - 1

questions.stem_text: A father went to pick up his son from his uncle’s house. He travelled a certain distance with a speed of 8 km/h and then he took a bus, which was traveling at 40 km/h for the remaining distance. Overall, it took him 20 hours to reach the destination. If the average speed of the entire journey is 25 km/h, find the distance he travelled by bus in km.

At the beginning of a journey, due to the breakdown of a car, its speed decreased by 30% and, thus, it took 50 minutes more to reach the destination. Find the actual time taken(in hours) by the car for the entire journey.
In a race, A gives B a start of 80 m. If A runs at 20% higher speed than B, it ends in a dead heat. Find the length of the race.

Bunty and Babloo run a 200-meter race, where Bunty beats Babloo by 30 meters. To do a favor to Babloo, Bunty starts 20 meters behind the starting line in a second 200 meter race. They both run at their earlier speeds. Which of the following is true in connection with the second race?

options.text: Bunty and Babloo reach the finishing line simultaneously

questions.stem_text: In a linear race among four friends, Praveen, Quresh, Ranveer, and Sachin, Praveen beats Quresh by 300 meters or 10 seconds. Ranveer beats Sachin by 30 seconds. If the ratio speeds of Praveen, Quresh, and Ranveer is 6 : 5 : 4, then by how much time will Sachin beat Ranveer if he triples his speed.

Two aeroplanes are flying towards each other on parallel paths. The speed of the wind is 10 kmph and the speed of the planes are 900 kmph and 500 kmph, respectively. If the two aeroplanes are 7000 km apart, then how long will it take them to cross each other? [Ignore the length of the aeroplanes]

options.text: Data insufficient as the direction of the wind is not given

questions.stem_text: Mr. Tarun completes the race at an average speed of 30 km/h. His average speed for the first stretch is twice of that of the second stretch and five times that of the last stretch. Find the speed over stretch C.

Mr. Jakyell completes the first stretch at the minimum speed and takes 1.5 time for stretch B. He takes 50% more time than the previous record to complete the race. What is Mr. Jakyell’s speed for the stretch C (use info from 1st question)?

Time, Speed & Distance.title: AIM 99+ IN CAT 2025 | ACE Time Speed And Distance - 2

questions.stem_text: Biswajit drove from his home to his office at 40 kmph in the morning. While returning he took a different path to avoid the evening traffic. This path is 10 km more than his onward journey. He drove half of the path at a speed of 30 kmph and rest at a speed of 50 kmph. His return journey took 19 minutes more than his onward journey. How much did he travel while returning from office?

Two motorboats, A and B are travelling to and fro in a moving river. In still water, the speed of motorboat A is 50% more than that of motorboat B. To cover ‘d’ km to and fro, motorboat A takes 20 minutes less than motorboat B. Instead if motorboat A covers (2d) km to and fro, motorboat A takes 16 minutes more than the time taken by motorboat B to cover ‘d’ km to and fro. How much time will the motorboat A takes to cover ‘d’ km to and fro?

Ram and Shyam simultaneously start running from the same point in opposite directions along the perimeter of a circular garden of circumference 600 m. If their first point of meeting is at a distance of 240 m in a counterclockwise direction from the starting point, what would be the distance of their third meeting point from the starting point in the clockwise direction?

Two friends named Ajay and Vijay simultaneously start swimming from points A and B respectively towards points B and A respectively with constant speed. On reaching points B and A respectively, they immediately turn back and swim towards their starting points with the same speed. They continue swimming this way. If their first meeting point is at a point 180 m away from A, where will be their second meeting point, if the distance between A and B is 300 m?

Train X and train Y travel at 54 km/h and 60 km/h respectively. The time taken by train X to completely cross a tree is 24 seconds. The time taken by train X and train Y to completely cross a railway platform is 90 seconds and 72 seconds respectively. What is the length (in meter) of train Y?

Three cars — A, B and C travel at the speed of 90, 60 and 50 km/h respectively. These cars start from the same point and in the same direction. Car A and Car C started at 9 AM and 6 AM respectively. If all the three cars meet at the same place at the same time, then at what time did Car B start?
In a 720 feet race, C beats B by 120 feet and B beats A by 360 feet. By what distance will C beat A?
A man rows 15 km in 1 hour in still water and in 90 minutes against the current. Find the time (in hours) taken by him to row 40 km with the current and return to the starting point.

There are three runners Aadit, Badal and Chahel at the same point. Aadit starts running at a speed of 6 m/s from a point. After 10 seconds, Badal starts running behind Aadit at a speed of 8 m/s. After 5 more seconds, Chahel also starts running behind Badal at a speed of 10 m/s. What is the distance (in m) between Chahel and Badal when Chahel catches Aadit if all of them are running along a straight line?

In a 100 m race, Asha beats Bina by 10 m even if she gives Bina a head start of 5 seconds. If Asha gives Bina a head start of 10 seconds in the same race, then Bina beats Asha by 20 m. What is the speed (in m/s) of Bina?

Time, Speed & Distance.title: AIM 99+ IN CAT 2025 | ACE Time Speed And Distance - 3

questions.stem_text: Arav participated in a 600 m race, during which he increased his speed by 20% after every 100 m that he covered. If he finished the race in 13 minutes and 18 seconds, what was his approximate speed (in kmph) for the first 100 m of the race?
For which of the following sets of speeds (in m/s) will two or more runners meet at a point between two consecutive poles?(use info from 8th question)

There are 360 poles, fixed symmetrically, all around a circular track. Four runners A, B, C and D, start simultaneously from the first pole and run, all in the same direction, around the track at speeds of a, b, c and d respectively. If all the four runners do not run in the same direction, then for which of the following sets of speeds (in m/s) is it not possible for two or more runners to meet at a point on the track between two consecutive poles?

Two trains started simultaneously, at 11:00 am, from Bangalore and Orissa, moving towards Orissa and Bangalore respectively, and crossed each Other at 2:00 pm. If the train starting from Orissa reached its destination 270 minutes before the other train, when did the train starting from Bangalore reach Orissa?

The vertical distance covered by a body falling freely under gravity is directly proportional to the square of the time for which it falls. If a body covers 300 m in the first 10 seconds of its free fall, what is the distance (in m) covered by the body in the next 10 seconds of its fall?

Every day on her way to office, Charitha crosses a Car coming from the opposite direction, at a certain point on the way. One day Charitha was late by 20 minutes and as a result she crossed the Car ten minutes after the usual time. What is the ratio of the speed of the Car to that of Charitha?

On a circular track, with center O, A starts at a point P and runs in the clockwise direction. At the same time, B starts at Q and runs in the anticlockwise direction. The angle POQ, measured in the clockwise direction, is 270. The distance covered by B by the time he meets A for the first time is equal to the distance covered by A between the first and second meetings. The fraction of the track length A covers in the first meeting is a/bwhere a and b are coprime, find a*b?

Anand, Bunty and Chintu were classmates and wanted to go to GRS Fantasy Park at Mysore right after their class got over. Bunty had an Hero Honda bike which could accommodate a maximum of two persons at a time and they wanted to reach the mall in the minimum time possible. They started simultaneously from their college, Bunty and Anand on the bike and Chintu on foot. Bunty dropped Anand at a certain point along the route to the mall, turned back and on his way met Chintu. He picked up Chintu and then traveled towards the mall such that all three of them reached the mall simultaneously. What is the total time (in minutes) taken by them to reach the Fanatasy Park from their college, if the speed of the bike was 60 km/hr, the walking speed of each of them was 6 km/hr and the distance between their college and the Fantasy Park was 26 km?

Usually, Ravi takes 180 minutes to drive from his house to his in-laws’ house. Last Friday, after covering 30% of the distance to his in-laws’ house, Ravi’s car developed an engine problem, which reduced his speed (when compared to his usual speed) by 16 km/hr for the remaining part of the journey. If it took Ravi a total of 204 minutes to cover the entire distance, find the distance (in km) between his house and his in-laws’ house.

Time, Speed & Distance.title: AIM 99+ IN CAT 2025 | ACE Time Speed And Distance - 4

questions.stem_text: The rear wheel of a motorbike is larger in diameter than its front wheel. The measure of their radii are 21 cm and 28 cm. The motorbike travelled 99 km in 1 hour and 15 minutes. Assuming that the speed of the bike didn’t change during the entire journey, the difference in the numbers of revolutions of the two wheels per minute is

Sita and Gita start running simultaneously from two opposite ends of a straight path towards each other. After crossing each other, Sita and Gita reach their destinations in four and nine minutes, respectively. The ratio of speeds of Sita to that of Gita is

Car-1 travels distance ‘D’ with speed ‘2s’ whereas, Car-2 travels distance ‘2D’ with speed ‘s’. The difference between their travel times is 45 minutes. The time (in hour) taken by Car-2 to travel distance ‘2D’ is

Two racers are running to and fro from the two opposite end points of a 500 metre long straight running track starting simultaneously from the two end points with uniform speeds of 3 m/s and 7 m/s. What will be the distance (in metres) of the slower runner from his starting point when he meets the faster runner for the third time?

A motorcyclist leaves Kolkata at 7 am and reaches Kharagpur at 10 am. Starting from 7:01 am, after every 10 minutes a car leaves Kharagpur and moves towards Kolkata. These cars cross the motorcyclist on the road from the opposite side. How many cars will cross the motorcyclist from the opposite direction, if the motorcyclist doubles his speed and the interval between two consecutive cars' leaving time is also doubled, keeping all other things the same as before?

Remus takes 30 seconds to finish riding an escalator if he walks on it at his normal speed in the same direction. He takes 18 seconds to finish riding the escalator if he walks at twice his normal speed in the same direction. If Remus decides to stand still on the escalator, then the time, in seconds, needed to finish riding the escalator is
A staircase has a total of 15 steps. If you can climb up one or two steps at a time, in how many ways can you reach the top of the staircase?

Two ships start from the same port, one sailing due South-East and the other sailing due South-West, both travelling at constant speeds, the speed of the latter being 7 kmph greater than that of the former. If, after five hours, they are 65 km apart, then the sum of their speeds, in kmph is

Raghu is going on a long drive of 600 km. His car gives him a uniform mileage of 15 km per L of petrol. At the start of the journey the car has 10 L of fuel. The capacity of the fuel tank is 20 L. After driving for 45 km, Raghu reads a road sign showing the distances from that point to five petrol stations ahead on the road. These distances are P-20 km, Q-100 km, R-250 km, S-315 km. If he wants to keep the average fuel content in his tank to be as low as possible while making only two stops for refueling, which of the following statements is true?

options.text: He stops for refueling at P and R
He fills 15 L at Q and then 20 L at S.
He fills 14 L at Q and then 16 L at S.

questions.stem_text: In a 1000 m race Akash, Manu, and Rohit are the three participants. Akash, Manu, and Rohit can complete the race in the same time if Akash gives Manu a start of 50 m and Rohit a start of 69 m. The start (in m), which Manu can give Rohit is:

Time, Speed & Distance.title: AIM 99+ IN CAT 2025 | ACE Time Speed And Distance - 5

questions.stem_text: A truck travelling at 90 km/h uses 33.33% more diesel to travel a distance than it does when it travels at a speed of 60 km/h. If the truck can travel 12 km per liter of diesel at 60 km/h speed, how far can the truck travel on 20 liters of diesel at a speed of 90 km/h?

Every day Neeru’s husband meets her at the city bus stand at 5:00 P.M. and drives her to their residence. One day she left early from the office and reached the bus stand at 4:00 P.M. She immediately started walking towards her home, met her husband coming from their residence on the way and they reached home 20 min earlier than usual time. For how long did she walk?

In a national park there was a lion and a tiger. In a specific time duration of t seconds, when lion moves 4 steps, tiger moves 7 steps, but the distance covered by lion is 5 steps is equal to the distance covered by tiger in 8 steps. If in 4t seconds, tiger moves 280 meters, find the distance (in meters) traveled by lion in same 4t seconds.

Ram and Shyam start from Karnal and Delhi towards Delhi and Karnal respectively at the same time. After they meet at Panipat on the way from Karnal to Delhi, Ram reduces his speed by 37.5% and goes to Karnal back and Shyam increased his speed by 37.5% and goes back to Delhi. If Ram takes 2 hours 23 minutes for the entire journey, what is the time taken(min) by Shyam for the entire journey?

Shanky started for his office from his home k min late, so he drove the car 160% of his usual speed and reached office k min early. Next day, he started 2k min early and wanted to reach office 3k min late. The speed he should travel is given by a/b, if his normal speed is x km/min,find a+b where a and b are co primes?

Ram and Shyam start simultaneously from P towards Q and from Q towards P respectively. They shuttle between P and Q indefinitely. They met for the first time at 88 km from Q. They meet at a point 44 km from P for the second time. If their speeds are in the ratio 3 : 2 respectively, then at what point they meet for the third time?

Two friends P and Q leave city K and L simultaneously and travel towards L and K at constant speeds. They meet at a point in between the two cities and after meeting it took P and Q, 72 min and 32 min respectively to reach their destinations. Find the time taken by Q to cover the entire journey between city K and L.

Two men A and B started walking towards each other simultaneously from P and Q respectively, which are 480 km apart. They meet after 12 hours. After the meeting A reduced his speed by 4 km/h and B increased his speed by 4 km/h. Both reached their destination points (Q for A and P for B) at the same time. Find the initial speed of B (in km/h).

Sonu and Monu leave city Hisar and Amritsar at 5 : 00 A.M. and travels towards Amritsar and Hisar respectively. Speed of Sonu is 72 km/h and sped of Monu is 108 km/h. Jatin leaves Hisar for Amritsar sometime later and travels at a speed of 90 km/h. If the distance between Hisar and Amritsar is 480 km and all three meet at the same point on the way, at the same time, then at what time did Jatin leaves Hisar?

Time, Speed & Distance.title: AIM 99+ IN CAT 2025 | ACE Time Speed And Distance -6

questions.stem_text: How many times do the hands of a clock coincide in a day?
At what time between 3 PM and 4 PM would the two hands of the clock be together?
At what time between 5 and 6 o’clock, do the minute and hour hands make an angle of 34 degree with each other
How many times in a day, the hands of a clock are straight?
What is the angle(in degrees) between the minute hand and the hour hand of a clock at 5:30?
By 20 minutes past 4, the hour hand has turned through how many degrees? If at the moment the clock is showing 12 PM.
An accurate clock shows 7 AM. Through how many degrees will the hour hand rotate when the clock shows 1 PM?

A house has two wall clocks, one in kitchen and one more in the bedroom. The time displayed on both the watches is 12 AM right now. questions.stem_text: A and B start a race of 600 m. When A reaches the 300 m line, B is 50 m behind. Immediately, C starts from that point (the 300 m point) in the same direction as A and B, while B and A continue to run in the same direction at constant speeds. When A reaches the finishing line, C is 32 m behind A. Had all three of them, that is, A, B, C, started from the initial starting point to the final finishing line, who would have won and by what distance among B and C (rounded off)?

On a Sunday morning, A starts walking towards the church for his “Sunday prayer”. The church bell rings once every 12 minutes 5 seconds, but Steve hears the second ring 10 minutes 55 seconds after the 1st ring. Find the speed of A in m/s, if the speed of sound is 330 meters per second.
In an 800 m race, if S gives G a start of 25 m, she beats her by 30 seconds, while if S gives G a start of 40 seconds, G beats S by 40 meters. Find the speed of G in m/s.
If the ratio of the speed of A to the speed of H = m : n, find m + n.
How many Bikes can finish the race in less than 36 hours?

A scientist wanted to create a bike that can be ridden with very high speeds using Bresnan technology, so he modelled 8 Bikes that are to be run using simulators to test their new maximum speed. Let the bike names be A, B, C, D, E, F, G, and H. They are running in lanes 1, 2, …… 8, respectively. The total length of the race is 705600 km. The speeds of A, B, …H, respectively, are proportional to the square of the respective lane numbers and C takes 100 hours to complete the race. (use this data for question 4,5,6)By how much distance will F beat G in the race?

Two friends — Prakash and Abhay — started running simultaneously from point J in the same direction along a straight running track. The ratio of the speeds of Prakash and Abhay was 3:8. three hours later, Abhay turned back and started running backwards at three-eighths of his original speed. He met Prakash at a distance of 33 km from the point where Prakash started. What was Prakash’s speed?

If the speed of a car increases in an Arithmetic Progression (AP) every hour and it takes 4 hours to cover a certain distance, then find the average speed of the car for this entire journey comes out to be speed in which hour

In a mile race, Akshay can be given a start of 256 m by Bhairav. If Bhairav can give Chinmay a start of 8 m in a 100 m dash, then who out of Akshay and Chinmay will win a race of two miles, and what will be the final lead given by the winner to the loser? (One mile is 1,600 m.)

questions.stem_text: SRK and KRK are running around a circular track in opposite direction. They both started simultaneously from the same point. Their 11th meeting point is same as their 29th meeting point. If the ratio of their speeds is k : 1 (k is a natural number), then how many values of k are possible?

Two persons A and B, start simultaneously from a point on a circular track and drive around the track in the same direction, with speeds of 36 km/h and 24 km/h respectively. Every time A overtakes B (only where on the track), both of them decrease their respective speeds by 2 km/h. If the length of the race track is 2 km, how many times do they meet before B comes to rest?

A, B and C are running around a circular part. They start at the same point and run in the same direction. A overtakes C, 5 times for every round that C makes. C overtakes B, 8 times for every round that B makes. What is the ratio of A’s speed to that of B’s?

Sonu and Monu started running simultaneously along a circular track from the same point. If they run in the same direction, they met for the first time after time ‘11t’ and if they run in the opposite direction they met for the first time after time ‘5t’. If Sonu’s speed is more than Monu’s speed, then Monu’s speed is how much % less than Sonu’s speed?

Two circular tracks T1 and T2 of radii 180 m and 120 respectively touch each other at point P. starting from point P at the same time, Mohan and Sohan are running on track T1 and T2 at speeds 24 km/h and 15 km/h respectively. The number of full rounds that Sohan will make before he meet Mohan again for the first time is? .

Three friends A, B, and C decide to run around a circular track. They start at the same time and run in the same direction. Speed of A is more than speed of B, which is more than the speed of C. when A finishers a lap, it is seen that C is as much behind B as B is behind A. When A completes 4 laps, C is at the exact same position on the circular track as B was when A finished 1 lap. If the ratio of speed of A, B and C is a : b : c, then what can be the value of a + b + c?

A, B, C, and D run along a circular track of length 5 km, starting from the same point at the same time in the clockwise direction. If they, run at a speed of 18 km/h, 15 km/h, 12 km/h and 9 km/h respectively, how much distance (in km) will D have run, when A, B, and C meet again for the first time at the starting point?

Two friends P and Q simultaneously start running around a circular track in the same direction. P runs at 12 m/s and Q runs at k m/s. If they cross each other at exactly three points on the circular track and k is a natural number less than 32, how may values of k are possible?

Find the headstart, that R can give to P in a 2400 m race (in m), so that both R and P finish the race at the same time?

In a 1200 m race, A can give B a headstart of 180 m or 15 sec and the race ends up in a dead heat. In a 600 m race, A can give C a headstart of 5.5 sec and the race ends up in a dead heat. In a 2500 m race, how many meters of headstart C can give to B, so that race ends up in a dead heat?

In a 1200 m race, A gives B a headstart of 180 m and still beats B by 180 m. In another race of 1200 m, A gives C a headstart of 240 m and still beats C by 240 m. In a 1680 m race, B will beat C by how many meters?

A takes 3 min 16 sec, while B takes 5 min 8 sec to complete a race. A beats B by 784 m in this race. If B’s speed is increased by 300% and A’s speed is increased by 100%, then B will beat A by how many meters in the same race?.

P, Q, and R all run in a linear race with uniform speeds. In a race of L meters, P beats Q by 120 meters and R by 240 meters. In same L meters race, Q beats R by 150 meters. Find the value of L.

In a race, Ram gives Shyam a headstart of 20 sec and beats Shyam by 200 m. In another race on the same track (and of same length) both start at the same time and Ram wins by 40 sec. If the length of the race track (in all cases) is 1200 m, then Shyam’s speed is how much percent less than Ram’s speed?

questions.stem_text: P, Q, and R starts walking from the same point. P and Q starts at 9 A.M. and R starts at 11 A.M. R takes over P at 2 P.M. and then doubles his speed and takes over Q at 4 P.M. P speed is how much % less than Q speed?

After another hour, Car B again turn back and changes its speed to 40 km/hr. Car B keeps on changing its speed (40 to 20 and vice-versa) and direction in this manner till the time it meet Car A. After how much time Car A and Car B will meet for the first time?

P travelled uniformly with average speed of 8 km/h, while Q travelled with varying speed as follows: In the first hour his speed was 5 km/h, in the second hour it was 7 km/h, in the third hour it was 9 km/h and so on. At what time will they meet each other?

questions.stem_text: A, B, and C started a business at the beginning of an year with investments of ₹ 3000 each. At the start of each month starting from the second month, A added ₹ 1500 each. At the start of 3rd, 5th, 7th, 9th and 11th month, B added ₹ 3000 each. At the start of 4th, 7th and 10th month, C added ₹ 4500 each. If the ratio of their profit share is p : q : r, then find the value of p + q + r.

A, B, and C started a business by investing ₹ 12000, ₹ 18000, and ₹ 24000 respectively. A added ₹ 3000 each at the start of 4th, 7th and 10th month. B added ₹ 3000 each at the start of 4th and 7th month, but withdrawn ₹ 9000 at the end of 9th month. C had withdrawn ₹ 3000 each at the end of 3rd and 6th month but added ₹ 3000 at the start of 10th month. If the total annual profit at the end of year is ₹ 15200, find the B’s share (in ₹).

Sohan, Mohan, and Rohan entered into a partnership with investments of ₹ 11100, ₹ 18500, and ₹ 33300 respectively. It was decided that Rohan will manage the business for whole year. But after some months due to personal reasons Rohan handed over his responsibility of managing the business to Mohan for the rest of the year. The person who manages the business gets 10% of the annual profit as commission. If the total income (Commission + share of profit) of Rohan at the end of the year is ₹ 21820 and the total annual profit is ₹ 40800, for how many months did Rohan managed the business?

P and Q started a business. They made a certain annual profit. P being a working partner received 16.66% of the annual profit as his salary and remaining profit was divided among P and Q in the ratio of their investments. Instead if the entire profit was divided in the ratio of their investment, Q would have received ₹ 6789 more than what he actually got. Find the actual share (in ₹) of Q.

Raj, Kumar and Hirani started a business with investments of ₹ 12000, ₹ 18000, and ₹ 24000 respectively. Both Kumar and Hirani left the business after k months. Out of the annual profit share, Raj got more than Kumar but less than Hirani. If k is an integer, find the value of k.

Rohan and Sohan started an enterprise by investing ₹ 64000 and ₹ 84000 respectively. Their agreement is to share half of the total profit equally and then share the remaining profit in the ratio of their investment. Instead if they had shared the entire profit in the ratio of their investments, Sohan would have got ₹ 2500 more than what he actually got. Find the total profit (in ₹).

A, B, and C got into a partnership for a year by investing ₹ 72000, ₹ 84000 and ₹ 96000 respectively. A is a working partner and got 25% of the total annual profit as annual salary and the remaining profit is divided among the three in the ratio of their investments. What is the amount of profit that B got (in ₹) as his share, if A got a total of ₹ 11700 (salary + share in profit)?

A started a firm with a capital of ₹ 35000. After 5 months, B joined him and invested ₹ 24000 in the firm. C was also added as a new partner after 8 months of commencement of firm with an individual investment of ₹ 49000. If at the end of the year, the total profit of the firm is ₹ 84000, find the share of C (in ₹).

Mukesh, Rakesh, and Sukesh started a business with investments in the ratio 2 : 3 : 5. Rakesh and Sukesh left the business after 6 and 9 months respectively, while Mukesh remained in the business for 12 months. If the total profit at the end of the year is ₹ 20300, find the share of Rakesh (in ₹).

A body with a mass of 3 grams and having a speed of 12 m/s has a kinetic energy of 216 joules. Find the kinetic energy (in joules) of a body whose mass is 7 grams and speed is 16 m/s?

Find the total number of apples distributed.

questions.stem_text: A foreign plant species is introduced on an island. The population of this species increases at a rate of 2 : 5 every month. Before the introduction, there were 18 million native plant species on the island, and 6 millions of foreign species is introduced on the island. The native plant population grows at a rate of 2 : 3 per month. Find the number of months required for the introduced species to surpass the native species population.

Find the amount of money (in paisa) by which A’s share exceeds D’s share.

Mohan, Rohan and Sohan are standing in a queue with Mohan is standing ahead of Rohan and Rohan is standing ahead of Sohan. There are 5 persons between Mohan and Rohan and 7 persons between Rohan and Sohan. The ratio of number of persons standing ahead of Mohan to the number of persons standing behind Sohan is 3 : 4. If the total number of persons in the queue is not more than 88, find the maximum number of persons standing behind Sohan.

If the total profit of company C and D together is Rs. 36000 more than the total profit of company A and B together, then find company E’s profit (in Rs.).

If number of coins received by each son (or would have received) in both cases are integers, then find the gain of A for the minimum possible value of K.

Find the ratio of the length of middle part (which is neither smallest nor the largest) to the length of the smallest part.

3880 is divided into three parts such that 11 times the first part, 14 times the second and 1.6 times the third are equal. Find the sum of the first and the third parts?

He can make a profit of 15% profit if he mixes coffee and chicory is 7:3 whereas the profit goes down to 10% if he mixes chicory and coffee in 1:3. Find out the ratio of the cost price per kg coffee to chicory.

If x < y < z and the price of alloy is Rs 30 per kg, then what would be the price per kg of the alloy if they are mixed in ratio x : z : y ?

More workers were hired such that their number doubled, but company decided to keep the total salary of its employees the same. If the ratio of the salaries of a manager and worker remains the same, what is the percentage decrease in the salary of the managers?

He gathered the three prisoners and told them that they will be asked to take sweets from a sweet box in a separate room in the ratio of their respective cell numbers. The prisoner from cell number 6, however, thought that he was the second one to enter the room after inmate from cell 4. What proportion of sweets were remaining at the end?

B then gave one-third of the number of gold coins he had with him to C and finally C have one-fifth of the number of gold coins he had with him to D. Finally, all the four boys had an equal number of gold coins. If initially the number of gold coins with A was 280 more than the number of gold coins with C, find the sum of the number of gold coins with B and D initially.

If the ratio of the amount with the 11th brother to the amount with the first brother is 12:1, then how much money (in Rs.) did the 7th brother get?
