Chanda brought 480 oranges and planned to sell them with a 8.33% profit. Due to heavy rain for 3 days, a few of the oranges were spoiled. In order to get 8.33% profit now, Chanda marks up the price of the remaining oranges by 30%. Find the number of unspoiled oranges.

Ronit selected the same shirt in two websites: one for Rs. 2800 on Highlow and another for Rs. 3360 on Flagslip. MDFC bank offers a discount of 12.5% on Flagslip and 7.14% on Highlow. But Flagslip allows another discount of 6.67% on all purchases above Rs. 3000. The highest price is how much percent more than the lowest price?

Sandeep brought 60 balls. All of them are either green or blue in colour. The price of the blue ball is Rs. 10 less than that of the green ball. Sandeep sold all the green balls for Rs. 875 and all the blue balls for Rs. 375. If all the prices are positive integers, then find the sum of the prices of one green ball and one blue ball.

Harshwardhan and Sudarshan had an unequal number of books to sell. In total, there were 85 books and each of them sold their books at a fixed price per unit. The total incomes incurred by them is in the ratio of 49 : 16. If they exchange their selling prices, then Harshwardhan and Sudarshan will earn Rs. 3136 and Rs. 5184, respectively. Find the cheaper price per book.

Naveen had 5 identical books; he sold exactly one book every day for 5 consecutive days. The profit earned each day is double the profit earned on the previous day. If he earns at the rate of Rs. 2 per Rs. 25 on the 1st day, find the overall profit % for the 5 days. (approximately)?

Nonu, Puppy, and Kallu secured 60%, 75%, and 85%, respectively, of the maximum marks in the maths test and 75%, 85%, and 90% of the maximum marks in the science test, respectively. If Nonu secured 6 marks more in the maths test than the science test and Puppy secured 18 marks less in the science test than the maths text, find the total number of marks secured by Kallu.

In a class, a student must choose one among statistics or economics as an additional subject. The number of students who choose statistics is 40% more than the number of students who choose economics. The number of students who appeared for the quiz test is 40% less than the number of students who did not appear for the quiz test. Express the number of students who choose statistics as a percentage of the students who participated in the quiz test?

Gopal invested his money in three banks in the ratio of 10 : 7 : 8. If each bank promised a growth of 20%, 28.56%, and 25% per year. If the overall growth after one year is X, and the same overall growth rate is observed in the second year, find the overall growth of 2 years combined.

Saurabh is playing a gambling game which has 3 rounds. For each round, Saurabh receives Rs. 2000 if he wins, but he must give away half of the total amount ha has at that point of time ie after receiving Rs 2000. If he wins all the 3 rounds, find 6 times the initial amount that Saurabh had if he finally has half of the initial amount.

SI & CI.title: AIM 99+ IN CAT 2025 | ACE SI AND CI - 1

questions.stem_text: What annual installment (in ₹) will discharge a debt of ₹ 31000 due after 5 years at 12% p.a. simple interest. Each installment is being paid at the end of the each year.

A sum was borrowed at 25% p.a. compound interest. It was repaid in three annual installments with each installment being paid at the end of the year. The 1st, 2nd, and 3rd installment was ₹2540, ₹3350 and ₹5250 respectively. Find the sum (in ₹)
The difference between the compound interest on a certain sum for third and fourth years at 12.5% p.a. (with interest being compounded annually) is ₹ 810. Find the principal (in ₹)

The compound interest and simple interest on a certain sum for two years are ₹ 1530 and ₹ 1440 respectively. The rate of interest is same for both simple interest and compound interest. What is the principle (in ₹)

Rohan borrows ₹ 64000 from Mohan at 20% simple interest. Rohan lends this money to Sohan at 20% compound interest (with interest being compounded annually) for two years. Rohan’s net income (interest received — interest paid) is ₹P. Had Rohan borrowed the same money for three years and lend it to Sohan for 3 years at same rate of interest, then his net income would have been ₹ Q. Find the value of (Q – P) (in ₹).
A particular sum of many amounts to ₹ 12,000 in 4 years and ₹ 96,000 in 7 years at a certain rate of compound interest with interest being compounded annually. Find the amount (in ₹) after 5 years

A principal amounts to ₹ 11440 at 10% p.a. simple interest after 3 years. What is the compound interest for two years on this principal at the same rate of interest (interest is being compound annually)?

Mohan invests a total of ₹ 1,27,000, part of it in scheme A at 12.5% p.a. and rest of it in scheme B at 33.33% p.a., both at compound interest (with interest being compounded annually). If after two years, the amount in the scheme B is ₹ 31,000 more than the amount in scheme A, then how much (in ₹) was invested in scheme B?

Rohan borrowed a sum at compound interest (with interest being compounded annually) and it amounts to ₹ 648000 at the end of two years and to ₹ 820125 at the end of 4 years. Find the principal (in ₹).

₹ 24000 was lent at compound interest (interest being compounded annually) for 3 years. The annual rate of interest for 1st, 2nd, and 3rd year were 12.5%, 20%, and 30% respectively. If it was instead lent at 25% p.a. simple interest for the same time, how much more/less interest would be realized?

SI & CI.title: AIM 99+ IN CAT 2025 | ACE SI AND CI - 2

questions.stem_text: Rahul borrowed a certain sum of money at a certain rate of simple interest. If instead the sum was borrowed at compound interest with interest being compounded annually, the total interest for the first two years would have increased by ₹ 2400, while the total interest for the first three years would have increased by ₹ 7500. Find the sum (in ₹).

If the difference between compound interest and simple interest on same principal at 20% p.a. for 3 years is ₹ 4800, then find the principal. Given that interest is being compounded annually in case of compound interest.

Mohan invests ₹ 50,000 at 12% p.a. compound interest (with interest being compounded semi-annually) and ₹ 40,960 at 50% p.a. compound interest (with interest being compounded quarterly). Sohan invested ₹ k at 10% simple interest. Both Mohan and Sohan invested for 1 year. If the total interest earned by both Mohan and Sohan is same, then find the value of k.

For a certain sum of ₹ P invested at R% p.a. (with interest being compound annually), the amount at the end of 12 years is ₹ 36990, and the amount at the end of 16 years is ₹ 110970. If the same sum of ₹ P is invested under simple interest for 10 years at 14% p.a., find the amount (in ₹).

Rohit borrowed ₹ 160160 at 20% p.a. compound interest (with interest being compounded annually). He planned to repay in two equal annual installments. But the actually repaid it in three equal annual installments. Find the difference (in ₹) between the value of each installment he had planned to pay and each installment that he actually paid. Given that each installment was paid at the end of the year in both the cases.

Rakesh borrowed ₹ 80,000 loan at 25% p.a. compound interest (with interest being compounded annually). He repaid ₹ x at the end of first year, ₹ 36000 at the end of second year and ₹ 48,000 at the end of third year and thereby cleared the loan. Find the value of x.

If a sum is lent at 40% p.a. compound interest (interest being compounded semi-annually) for one year, it would fetch an interest which is ₹ 5784 less than it would fetch if it is lent at the same rate of interest with interest being compounded quarterly . Find the sum (in ₹).

A sum of ₹ 25600 invested at simple interest doubles itself in 8 years at R% p.a. If ₹ 25600 is invested at 2R% p.a. compound interest, with interest being compounded annually, what will be the amount (in ₹) after three years?

KRK deposits a sum of ₹ P in a bank at R% p.a. with interest being compounded annually. The sum becomes ₹ 81P after 4 years. Instead, if interest had been compounded semi-annually at same rate of interest, what would KRK would have received terms of P after 4 years?

A sum at certain rate of compound interest (with interest being compounded annually) amounts to ₹ 3250 in two years and when lent at twice the previous rate of interest amounts to ₹ 4680 in the same time. What is the rate (in %) at which the sum was initially lent?

SI & CI.title: AIM 99+ IN CAT 2025 | ACE SI AND CI - 3

questions.stem_text: Rohan borrowed ₹ 135000 loan from a bank at 10% p.a. compound interest, with interest being compounded annually. He repaid the loan in 3 annual installment which are in arithmetic progression. He ends up paying a total of ₹ 162000. Find the value of last installment (in ₹).

Sohan invested ₹ k at R% p.a. compound interest, with interest being compounded annually. If the compound interest for third and fourth year are ₹ 6348 and ₹ 7300.2 respectively, then find the value of k.

Sonu invested ₹ 56,000 for 7 years at 8% p.a. compound interest, with interest being compounded semi-annually. Monu invests ₹ k for 6 years at 8% p.a. compound interest, with interest being compound semi-annually and the reinvests the entire amount received at the end of 6 years for one year at 12% simple interest. If the amount received by both at the end of 7 years is same, then find the value of k.

Mukesh has an initial capital of ₹ 48000. Out of this, he invests ₹ 18000 at 7.8 p.a. in bank P, ₹ 16000 at 8.3% p.a. in bank Q and the remaining amount at x% p.a. in bank R, each rate being simple interest. His combined annual interest income from these investments is equal to 8.375% of his total initial capital. If he had invested his entire capital in bank R at x% p.a. simple interest, his annual interest income (in ₹ ) would be?

Rohan took a loan of ₹ 240000 at 12% p.a. compounded interest, with interest being compounded semi-annually. He repaid ₹ 19664, ₹ 60900 and ₹ k at the end of first, second and third year respectively to clear the entire loan. Find the value of k.

Mohit took a loan of ₹ k at 25% p.a. compound interest with interest being compounded annually. He repaid the entire loan in 4 annual installments with each installment being paid at the end of the year. First, second, third and fourth installment was of ₹ 20,000, ₹ 22,500, ₹ 41250 and ₹ 50,000 respectively. Find the value of k.

Rohan deposits ₹ k with a bank at R% p.a. compound interest with interest being compounded annually and it becomes ₹ 32k in 25 years. If he had invested the same amount at R% p.a. simple interest for 25 years, what would be the amount?

A person invested ₹ 15360 at compound interest at 200% p.a. with interest being compounded quarterly for 3 years. Total amount received from this investment after 3 years is further invest under 66.66% compound interest, with interest being compound semi-annually for 3 years. Find the total amount at the end of 6 years (in ₹).

A certain sum is invested under compound interest, with interest being compound annually. The compound interest of second year was ₹ 1800 more than the compound interest of first year. If the rate of interest becomes 4 times the original rate, then the compound interest of second year will be how much more than that the compound interest of first year?

Rohan invested 37.5% of his capital at 8%, 18.75% of his capital at 12%, 31.25% of his capital at 6%, and remaining capital at 9%, each rate being simple interest p.a., then the minimum number of integral years required for the cumulative interest income from these investments to become equal to or exceeds twice of his initial capital is?

SI & CI.title: AIM 99+ IN CAT 2025 | ACE SI AND CI - 4

questions.stem_text: A sum of money is deposited at a certain rate of compound interest. If simple interest instead of compound interest was reckoned, then the interest for the first two years will be ₹ 300 less, and for the first three years, it will be ₹ 918 less. Calculate the simple interest on the same principal for 3 years at the same annual rate of interest.
If the compound interest earned on a certain sum for 2 years at 12% per annum is ₹ 1272, what will be the simple interest at the same rate for the same period?

Mr. A deposited two equal sums at simple but different rates of interest in 2 different schemes and withdrew both after 4 years and received 9 times the sum he deposited. Another person, Mr. B, deposited the same amount at the same time in the same schemes with Mr. A. However, he waited till the end and got 12 times the sum deposited. Mr. B investment was for how many years?

Anderson lent P and Q the same sum of money for the same length of time at the same interest rate. He gave P a loan on which compound interest is calculated for the first two years half yearly and simple interest is calculated every year after that. But he gave Q a loan on which simple interest is computed, and the sum triples in five years. If the difference between the total interest earned from A and B is ₹ 312384, then determine the total amount (in ₹ ) lent to A and B.

Sahil deposited ₹ 100,000 in a bank at 16% per annum, compounded annually for two years. He wanted to keep the amount deposited further, but at the end of the two years, there was a new law in place according to which, for any amount in the account that date onwards, any annual interest greater than ₹ 7500 would be taxed. Also, the rate of interest was reduced to 10% per annum for that year. For the next year, what minimum amount (in ₹ ) should Sahil withdraw from his account to ensure that he avoids paying the tax? (Assume that his account had zero balance before he deposited ₹ 100000)

Given that uranium-238 isotopes decays at a constant rate in such a way that uranium in it reduces to 50% in 4242 years. Find the age of a piece of microelectronic material in which the uranium is only 6.25% of the original.

A sum of ₹ 50,000 was lent at compound interest for 2 years, with interest being compounded annually. The annual rate of interest for first and second years was 30% and 50% respectively. Find the total compound interest (in ₹ ) at the end of 2 years.

An individual has 116600 to invest. Three portions of his money were divided. First, he invested at 10% SI for a year. Second, he invested at 14% SI for 2 years and Third part was invested at 18% SI for 3 years. He received an equal amount of simple interest from each of the three investments. Find the least part of his investment’s worth (in ₹ ).

Jugal deposited a certain sum in a bank at compound interest at a certain rate of interest with interest being compound annually. The sum amounts to ₹ 999 at the end of 22 years and ₹ 10989 at the end of 33 years. Find the value of the initial sum multiplied by 242 (in ₹ )

K invested a certain amount for two years under compound interest compounded annually and received ₹ 1872. If she had invested the same amount for six years at the same rate of interest compounded annually, she would have received ₹ 29952. What amount (in ₹ ) did she invest?
If under compound interest, a certain sum becomes twenty seven times itself in 27 years, and after N more years, it becomes 211 times itself, then what is absolutely true about N?
